data.title: Orbion, a general-purpose robotics network, announced the completion of its deployment and the opening of its subscription period.
data.description: Orbion, the universal robot network, has been successfully deployed on the BSC mainnet and is now officially operational. Orbion aims to build an open, transparent, and scalable network environment, supporting global users in managing the entire lifecycle of universal robots, covering design and construction, asset ownership, operation and maintenance, and continuous iteration and upgrades. Orbion also announced that its platform's native token, ORB (Orbion Token), is now available for a 72-hour public offering. The ORB token is the core credential for network governance, service payments, and value transfer.

data.title: Bank of Thailand Unlikely to Ease Policy After February Rate Cut
data.description: The Bank of Thailand has indicated that it is unlikely to further ease its monetary policy following an unexpected rate cut in February. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the central bank's stance amid ongoing economic assessments. The decision comes as the bank evaluates the impact of its previous actions on the economy. This move suggests a cautious approach to future policy adjustments, reflecting the bank's focus on maintaining economic stability.

data.title: Italy's March CPI Preliminary Rate Falls Short of Expectations
data.description: Italy's consumer price index (CPI) for March showed a preliminary monthly increase of 0.5%, according to Jin10. This figure falls below the anticipated 0.7% and matches the previous month's rate of 0.70%. The data suggests a slower-than-expected rise in consumer prices, which could influence economic forecasts and monetary policy decisions in the country.

data.title: Israeli Military Prepared for Extended Operations
data.description: The Israeli military has announced its readiness to continue operations in the coming weeks. According to Odaily, a spokesperson for the Israeli Defense Forces confirmed the preparedness for ongoing actions. The statement comes amid heightened tensions in the region.

data.title: KB Card of South Korea partners with Avalanche to launch a "hybrid" stablecoin credit card
data.description: KB Kookmin Card, one of South Korea's largest credit card companies, announced on Tuesday that it will partner with Avalanche to build a hybrid stablecoin payment model. This follows KB's announcement in January of a patent application covering a hybrid payment system that connects existing credit cards to digital wallets, allowing customers to use both stablecoins and credit limits on their cards simultaneously. The latest announcement from the credit card company states that it will collaborate with Avalanche to design a credit card payment model on a public blockchain. Furthermore, KB stated that it is working with digital asset infrastructure company OpenAsset to develop a comprehensive stablecoin system for top-ups, payments, and settlements. According to previous announcements from the company, the card will automatically prioritize using the stablecoin balance in the digital wallet linked to the credit card. When the stablecoin balance is insufficient, the remaining amount will be deducted from the credit card. (The Block)

data.title: Israeli military spokesman: We are prepared to continue operations in the coming weeks.
data.description: Israeli military spokesman: We are prepared to continue operations in the coming weeks. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitget has added TQQQ and SQQQ stock contracts, supporting leverage up to 20x.
data.description: According to an official announcement, to meet market demand for trading in the US technology sector, Bitget stock contracts have now launched two popular options: TQQQ (3x long Nasdaq 100 ETF) and SQQQ (3x short Nasdaq 100 ETF). These contracts support leverage up to 20x. For more details, please refer to the Bitget official platform.

data.title: Monero Releases RandomX v2.0 Upgrade
data.description: Monero has announced the release of its RandomX v2.0 upgrade. According to Foresight News, this marks a significant iteration since RandomX was first launched on Monero's mainnet in 2019. The previous version, RandomX v1, primarily relied on simple logical operations such as XOR for register mixing. The new v2.0 version introduces more complex AES encryption and decryption circuits, enhancing the algorithm's capabilities.

data.title: Bitcoin's Future Highs and Market Sentiment Analyzed by Experts
data.description:
Bitcoin's future price trajectory remains a topic of debate among experts. According to BlockBeats, renowned trader and chart analyst Peter Brandt, who accurately predicted Bitcoin's 2018 crash, holds a pessimistic view on Bitcoin reaching new highs by 2026. Brandt suggests that a new peak might not occur until the second quarter of 2027, emphasizing that his predictions are speculative. On the prediction market platform Polymarket, the probability of Bitcoin reaching $120,000 by 2026 is estimated at only 15%.

Regarding this year's low, Brandt noted that the $60,000 level on February 6 might not be the lowest for the year. He anticipates a potential retest of this level, or slightly lower, in September or October, marking the bear market cycle's low point and the start of a new bull market. Despite his cautious outlook, Brandt maintains his long-term belief in Bitcoin as a store of wealth, though he remains neutral or bearish on other cryptocurrencies.

Bitcoin analyst Willy Woo commented on March 17 that, from a liquidity perspective, Bitcoin has completed about one-third of its bear market. SkyBridge Managing Partner Anthony Scaramucci also remarked last week that Bitcoin is currently in the bear phase of its four-year cycle, suggesting that long-standing whale investors might create a self-fulfilling prophecy.

On the sentiment front, spot Bitcoin ETFs ended a four-week streak of net inflows, recording a net outflow of $296.18 million last week. The Crypto Fear and Greed Index reported a score of 8 on Monday, indicating a state of "extreme fear" since March 20.

data.title: South Korean Gang Member Sentenced to Eight Years for Bitcoin Misuse
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). A member of South Korea's "Seven Stars Gang" has been sentenced to eight years in prison by the Busan District Court, as reported by DL News. In 2019, the individual was entrusted by a friend to convert approximately $332,000 worth of Bitcoin into cash for a 0.5% commission. However, he squandered the entire amount on illegal gambling websites without returning any funds, prompting his friend to report the incident to the authorities.

During his time on the run, the gang member escalated his criminal activities by providing money laundering services to a fraud syndicate. This group defrauded victims of approximately $6.4 million over more than a year.

data.title: Netanyahu Claims Progress in War with Iran
data.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that the conflict with Iran is progressing towards its objectives, although this may not be reflected in terms of time. According to NS3.AI, Netanyahu emphasized that both Israel and the United States have caused substantial damage to Iran's nuclear program.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 67,000 USDT with a 1.21% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 31, 2026, 08:40 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 67,000 USDT and is now trading at 66,995.171875 USDT, with a narrowed 1.21% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: A new address withdrew 1,000 BTC from Binance, worth $67.25 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a new address withdrew 1,000 BTC from Binance, worth $67.25 million.

data.title: Iranian Military Warns Against Potential U.S. Ground Offensive
data.description:
The Iranian Armed Forces have issued a stern warning in response to the possibility of a U.S. ground offensive. According to Odaily, a spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Command stated on March 31 that Iran's military is prepared to decisively counter any aggression, vowing to 'cut off the legs of any invader and leave them in disgrace.'

The statement criticized the United States and Israel for underestimating Iran's capabilities, suggesting that this miscalculation not only damages their reputation but also leads them toward a path of destruction. The spokesperson accused the U.S. and Israel of living in a fantasy, believing that propaganda, military displays, and targeted assassinations of civilians, scientists, and military leaders would force Iran into submission. However, the statement emphasized that Iran has never surrendered to its enemies throughout history.

Furthermore, the statement addressed the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz, asserting that any ambitions by the U.S. and Israel to control the waterway are futile and will remain unfulfilled.

data.title: European Gas Prices Decline Amid Speculation of U.S. Withdrawal from Iran Conflict
data.description:
European gas prices have experienced a decline as traders assess the possibility of an early U.S. exit from the ongoing conflict in Iran. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the market's reaction to geopolitical developments that could influence energy supply dynamics.

The speculation surrounding the U.S. involvement in the Iran war has led to fluctuations in energy markets, with traders closely monitoring any signs of a shift in U.S. foreign policy. The potential withdrawal could impact global energy supply chains, affecting prices and availability.

Market analysts are evaluating the implications of such a move, considering the broader geopolitical landscape and its effects on European energy markets. The situation remains fluid, with traders keeping a close watch on developments that could alter the current trajectory of gas prices.

As the situation unfolds, the energy sector is poised for potential changes, with stakeholders assessing the risks and opportunities presented by the evolving geopolitical scenario.

data.title: Cryptocurrency investment firm Keyrock has completed its Series C funding round, led by SC Ventures.
data.description: Belgian digital asset services company Keyrock has completed a Series C funding round led by Standard Chartered's SC Ventures, valuing the company at $1.1 billion, with Ripple participating as an existing investor. The round is still open for further funding, with a maximum size of $100 million. The new funds will be used to strengthen the balance sheet, expand the product line, and pursue acquisition opportunities. Founded in 2017, Keyrock provides market-making, asset management, OTC trading, and options services, operating on more than 80 centralized and decentralized exchanges, and employs over 200 people worldwide. Last September, the company expanded its asset and wealth management business through the acquisition of Turing Capital.

data.title: Philippine Peso and Indian Rupee Decline Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure
data.description: The Philippine peso has depreciated to 60.8 per dollar, while India's rupee fell to an intraday low of 95.2, as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed to commercial traffic. According to NS3.AI, the peso's decline in March has surpassed 5%. The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas stated that its intervention is focused on moderating large fluctuations rather than maintaining a specific exchange rate level. Meanwhile, the Indian rupee has experienced an 11% drop over the fiscal year, marking its most significant decline since 2011-12. The Reserve Bank of India has imposed a cap on banks' net open positions in the onshore forex market at $100 million per day, effective April 10.

data.title: Keyrock Secures Series C Funding at $1.1 Billion Valuation
data.description: Keyrock has successfully raised a Series C funding round, achieving a valuation of $1.1 billion. According to NS3.AI, the financing was led by SC Ventures. The round remains open and has the potential to reach a total of $100 million. Keyrock plans to utilize the new capital to enhance its balance sheet, expand its range of services, and explore acquisition opportunities.

data.title: Trezor Transitions Firmware Architecture to Modular Platform
data.description: Trezor's firmware product lead, Jan Setina, announced at the EthCC[9] conference that the team is transitioning its firmware architecture from a monolithic structure to a modular application platform. According to Foresight News, this initiative aims to address previous structural bottlenecks, such as slow integration speeds and hardware storage limitations, caused by the internal team's need to review all code. Developers can now use Trezor's SDK and simulator to independently develop, publish, and maintain applications. The modular firmware supports dynamic loading of applications as needed, with each application running in an isolated sandbox environment. This ensures that vulnerabilities in a single application do not affect others, enhancing security while increasing the flexibility of functional expansion.

data.title: Linea Introduces Yield Enhancement Feature
data.description: Linea has announced the launch of a new yield enhancement feature. According to Foresight News, the platform has converted idle bridged Ethereum into sustainable ecosystem liquidity. This development does not require the introduction of new tokens, rebasing, or borrowing capital.
